$comment: Neutral shipped <dlfcn.h> (POSIX) — c16 (SQLite extension loading, sqlite3_load_extension via os_unix unixDlOpen/Sym/Close). dlopen/dlsym/dlclose/dlerror + RTLD_NOW + RTLD_GLOBAL (the only two SQLite references). Verified against glibc bits/dlfcn.h + Darwin dyld/dlfcn.h: the prototypes are IDENTICAL on both. ★ RTLD_GLOBAL DIVERGES per object-format and there is a COLLISION TRAP — 0x100=256 is RTLD_GLOBAL on glibc but RTLD_FIRST on Darwin; 0x8 is RTLD_DEEPBIND on glibc but RTLD_GLOBAL on Darwin — so RTLD_GLOBAL MUST be a per-format `variants` (elf 256 / macho 8), never flat. RTLD_LAZY=1 + RTLD_NOW=2 AGREE → flat. RTLD_LOCAL diverges too (elf 0 / macho 4) → variant (shipped for completeness though SQLite uses NOW|GLOBAL). The dl* symbols: on Linux glibc 2.34+ MERGED libdl into libc.so.6 (the WSL run target is modern glibc) → library elf=libc.so.6; on macOS they're in libSystem (dyld-backed). Gated availableObjectFormats:[elf,macho].
